//: Class descriptors for libdisplay.so. See RecoverWistronClasses.java
for the schema. Offsets and slot numbers are byte offsets, not
indices — they match what shows up in the existing decomp.

classes.name: IIC_INTF
classes.//: Abstract transport base. Every chip-specific subclass overrides
a subset of these slots. Field offsets are intentionally sparse
— we only name what we've reverse-engineered.

0x20.name: open_pid
0x20.//: (IIC_INTF*, u16 vid, u16 pid, u32 idx, u8 speed)

0x58.name: check_image
0x58.//: (IIC_INTF*, u8* src, u32 size, u32* blocks, u32* blksize, u32* sigsize) -> u8

0x68.name: get_block_data
0x68.//: (IIC_INTF*, u32 src_idx, u32 count, u8* out) -> u8

0xf0.name: read_seq
0xf0.//: (IIC_INTF*, u8 slave, u8 reg, u8 count, u8* out) -> u8 — read N i2c bytes starting at reg

0x120.name: write_byte
0x120.//: (IIC_INTF*, u8 slave, u8 reg, u8 val) -> u8 — write single i2c byte

classes.name: REALTEK_API
classes.//: RealTek panel-scaler chip access on top of IIC_INTF. The
concrete class is `MStarISP` derived; REALTEK_API is the
chip-specific SPI WP register access bytes used by

fields.name: jedec_id
fields.//: filled by spi_read_jedec_id (3-byte 0x9F op + 1-byte 0xAB RES op packed)

fields.name: iic
fields.//: transport — most calls dispatch through here
